tasks.name: Activation Patching Pilot: 9 Core Words
tasks.description: Validate whether 9 persistent core words (eight, lower, liked, offer, often, etc.) represent genuine hierarchy-driven absorption via activation patching. Suppress child feature and measure parent recovery rate.

pilot.pass_criteria: Recovery rate > 0.5 indicates genuine absorption; < 0.2 indicates metric artifact

visualizations.description: Activation patching recovery rates for 9 core words

tasks.name: Random SAE Baseline Activation Patching
tasks.description: Run same activation patching experiment on random SAE baseline to compare recovery rates. If trained SAE shows higher recovery, it suggests training optimizes genuine absorption, not just metric sensitivity.

pilot.pass_criteria: Trained SAE recovery > Random SAE recovery supports H7 interpretation

visualizations.description: Trained SAE vs Random SAE recovery rate comparison

tasks.name: Dead Feature Ratio Stratified Analysis
tasks.description: Analyze whether activation patching results differ for features with L0 > threshold (active) vs L0 = 0 (dead). High dead ratio (89-99%) may invalidate some prior conclusions.

pilot.pass_criteria: Results stratified by L0 show different patterns

visualizations.description: L0 vs recovery rate colored by dead/active status

tasks.name: Activation Patching Full: Extended Word Set
tasks.description: Extend activation patching to full set of 26 first-letter features. Validate whether the 9 core words are representative or outliers.

pilot.pass_criteria: Recovery patterns consistent with pilot; >50% words show genuine absorption

tasks.name: Architecture Comparison Pilot (JumpReLU vs TopK)
tasks.description: Pilot comparison of JumpReLU vs TopK architectures on activation patching. Must use matched training data/dict size to avoid confounding. EXPLORATORY - may skip if time insufficient.

pilot.pass_criteria: Significant difference between architectures after controlling for training data

visualizations.description: Architecture comparison: JumpReLU vs TopK recovery rates

tasks.name: Figure Generation for Paper
tasks.description: Generate final figures for paper: activation patching results, trained vs random comparison, dead feature analysis.

visualizations.description: Combined figure with recovery rates, comparisons, and stratified analysis

metadata.experiment_rationale: Activation patching validation was recommended for 3 consecutive reviews but never executed. This is the ONLY experiment that can distinguish genuine hierarchy-driven absorption from metric artifact.
metadata.key_question: Do the 9 persistent core words show genuine absorption (parent recovers when child suppressed) or metric artifact (parent never fired)?
